steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage contrasts diadromous fish (obligate migrators between freshwater and marine biomes) with euryhaline fish (salt-tolerant but non-migrating). The blank must express how diadromous fish are distinguished from euryhaline ones.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean distinguished or separated from — marking the line between obligate migrators and tolerant non-migrators. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — demarcated from: "can be demarcated from those that are merely euryhaline" — demarcated means clearly separated or distinguished. This captures the distinction the passage draws. ✓
  • Choice B — reconstituted as: "can be reconstituted as" — reconstituted means reformed or restructured. The passage is not describing transformation. ✗
  • Choice C — derived from: "can be derived from" — derived means originating from. The passage says nothing about diadromous fish originating from euryhaline ones. ✗
  • Choice D — conflated with: "can be conflated with" — conflated means wrongly merged or confused. The passage is doing the opposite: clarifying their difference. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
